steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find the interior angle at R using the linear pair
steps.content:
Since S, R, and Q are collinear, angles ∠QRP and ∠PRS form a linear pair on the line and are supplementary.
$$∠QRP + ∠PRS = 180^{\circ}$$
$$∠QRP = 180^{\circ} - 163^{\circ} = 17^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property to find ∠QPR
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in triangle PQR equals 180°:
$$∠QPR + ∠PQR + ∠QRP = 180^{\circ}$$
$$∠QPR + 132^{\circ} + 17^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
$$∠QPR = 31^{\circ}$$
That matches Choice B — $31^\circ$.
